practice.name: Problem 6.3.1 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: If you toss four coins, in how many ways will there be at least 1 head?

practice.name: Problem 6.3.2 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: If you toss four coins, in how many ways will there be at least 2 heads?

practice.name: Problem 6.3.3 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many three digit numbers contain the digit 5 at least once?

practice.name: Problem 6.3.4 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many 3 digit numbers have at least 2 digits that are the same?

practice.name: Problem 6.3.5 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many ways are there to select a group of 3 people from 5 women and 3 men if there must be at least 1 man in the group?

practice.name: Problem 6.3.7 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many 4 digit numbers are not palindromes? A palindrome is a number that reads the same forward and backward.

practice.name: Problem 6.3.11 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: There are 9 different flavors of ice cream at an ice cream shop. Joe wants to buy 2 or 3 scoops of different ice cream flavors. He doesn’t like 2 of the flavors Mango and Chocolate together so won’t buy them together. How many ways can he buy ice cre

practice.name: Problem 6.3.14 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: There are 10 slips of paper numbered 1 to 5 in a bag, each equally likely to be drawn. 87

practice.name: Problem 6.3.15 (Mastering AMC 8)
practice.statement: You have 6 identical pears and 5 identical apples. You choose 4 fruits at random. What is the probability of picking at least 1 pear? Answers 6.2 77 6.3 5 8 6.4 902 6.7 189/ 256 6.3.1 15 6.3.2 11 6.3.3 252 6.3.4 252 6.3.5 46 6.3.6 3 10 6.3.7 8910 6.3
